songs.title: Uptown Funk
songs.why: A modern classic of American funk-pop that channels the Minneapolis sound of Prince and James Brown-era showmanship — one of the best-selling digital singles of all time.

songs.title: Roar
songs.why: Arena-sized American pop optimism: a self-empowerment anthem that topped charts worldwide and embodies the polished maximalism of the US pop machine.

songs.title: All of Me
songs.why: A piano soul ballad in the great American R&B tradition — Legend's voice carries the lineage of gospel and Motown into the streaming era.

songs.title: Shape of You
songs.why: The face of the modern British singer-songwriter boom — for years the most-streamed song in Spotify history, written by a busker from Suffolk.

songs.title: Someone Like You
songs.why: British soul balladry at its rawest; Adele's heartbreak anthems continue the UK's long line of world-conquering vocalists from Dusty Springfield to Amy Winehouse.

songs.title: Perfect
songs.why: A modern British wedding-song standard, proof of the UK's outsized role in global pop songwriting.

songs.title: Gangnam Style
songs.why: The song that put K-pop on the world map — the first video in YouTube history to reach one billion views, satirising the flashy Gangnam district of Seoul.

songs.title: Dynamite
songs.why: BTS became the biggest boy band on Earth and the flagship of the Korean Wave (Hallyu); Dynamite was the first all-English single to send them to #1 on the US charts.

songs.title: DDU-DU DDU-DU
songs.why: The signature hit of the world's biggest girl group — K-pop's precision choreography, fashion and genre-blending sound in one video.

songs.title: Dernière Danse
songs.why: A global streaming phenomenon in French — its sweeping, chanson-inflected melancholy carries the torch of Édith Piaf into modern pop.

songs.title: Papaoutai
songs.why: Francophone pop's great modern storyteller: a danceable song about an absent father that dominated charts across the French-speaking world.

songs.title: Alors on danse
songs.why: The song that made French-language electro-pop a continental force again — a wry anthem about dancing through hard times.

songs.title: Hips Don't Lie
songs.why: Colombia's most famous musical export at her peak — cumbia horns and Barranquilla carnival rhythms smuggled into one of the century's biggest global hits.

songs.title: Mi Gente
songs.why: Medellín's J Balvin led reggaeton's second global wave; Mi Gente became a worldwide #1 and a statement that Spanish-language pop needs no translation.

songs.title: La Bicicleta
songs.why: Two generations of Colombian stars celebrating vallenato — the accordion folk music of the Caribbean coast — in a love letter to their home region.

songs.title: Idol
songs.why: The sound of modern J-pop's anime symbiosis — an Oshi no Ko theme that became the first Japanese-language song to top the Billboard Global chart.

songs.title: Lemon
songs.why: One of the best-selling Japanese songs of the digital era, by a Vocaloid-producer-turned-superstar — grief rendered as immaculate pop craft.

songs.title: Sukiyaki (Ue o Muite Arukō)
songs.why: The only Japanese-language song ever to top the US Billboard Hot 100 (1963) — a bittersweet classic that introduced Japanese pop to the world.

songs.title: Con Altura
songs.why: Rosalía reinvented flamenco for the streaming age — Con Altura fuses Andalusian tradition with reggaeton and won Video of the Year at the Latin Grammys.

songs.title: Bailando
songs.artist: Enrique Iglesias ft. Descemer Bueno, Gente de Zona
songs.why: Spain's biggest pop export bridging the Spanish-speaking world — flamenco guitar over Cuban rhythms in one of the most-viewed Spanish-language videos ever.

songs.title: Garota de Ipanema
songs.why: The definitive bossa nova song — born on a Rio beach, it became one of the most recorded songs in history and the sound of Brazil to the world.

songs.title: Mas Que Nada
songs.artist: Sérgio Mendes ft. The Black Eyed Peas
songs.why: Jorge Ben's samba standard, carried around the globe by Sérgio Mendes — this 2006 remake introduced Brazil's most joyful groove to a new generation.

songs.title: Ai Se Eu Te Pego
songs.why: Sertanejo — Brazil's country music — going global: footballers' goal celebrations turned this party song into a worldwide phenomenon.

songs.title: El Rey
songs.why: The unofficial anthem of ranchera music, sung by 'El Rey de la Música Ranchera' himself — mariachi pride, defiance and heartbreak in three minutes.

songs.title: Nunca Es Suficiente
songs.artist: Los Ángeles Azules ft. Natalia Lafourcade
songs.why: Mexican cumbia royalty meets the country's finest modern folk voice — proof that cumbia sonidera is the heartbeat of Mexican celebrations.

songs.title: Eres
songs.why: From Mexico's most beloved rock band, a tender standard of rock en español that generations of Mexicans know by heart.

songs.title: 99 Luftballons
songs.why: Cold-War Berlin in a pop song: a German-language protest hit about balloons triggering nuclear war that conquered charts on both sides of the Atlantic in 1983.

songs.title: Du Hast
songs.why: The flagship of Neue Deutsche Härte — Rammstein made industrial metal sung in German a global stadium spectacle.

songs.title: Major Tom (Coming Home)
songs.why: A synth-pop landmark of the Neue Deutsche Welle (German New Wave) that reimagined Bowie's astronaut and still echoes through pop culture.

songs.title: Nel blu dipinto di blu (Volare)
songs.why: Italy's most famous song — the 1958 Sanremo winner that won the first-ever Grammy for Record of the Year and made 'Volare' a word the whole world sings.

songs.title: Con te partirò
songs.why: Modern Italy's operatic pop ambassador — the crossover aria that carried the bel canto tradition into stadiums and wedding aisles worldwide.

songs.title: Zitti e Buoni
songs.why: The Eurovision 2021 winner that made a Roman glam-rock band a global act — Italian-language rock storming the world's playlists.

songs.title: Jai Ho
songs.why: The Oscar-winning anthem by India's most celebrated composer — Bollywood's exuberant sound distilled into a worldwide victory chant.

songs.title: Naatu Naatu
songs.why: The first song from an Indian film to win the Academy Award for Best Original Song — Telugu cinema's explosive energy and dance culture on the world stage.

songs.title: Calm Down
songs.why: The biggest Afrobeats crossover in history — a Benin City artist's melody that spent a record-breaking run on global charts and cemented Nigeria as pop's new powerhouse.

songs.title: Last Last
songs.why: The self-styled African Giant turning heartbreak into a continental anthem — Afro-fusion built on Nigerian pidgin and a Toni Braxton sample.

songs.title: Essence
songs.why: The first Nigerian song to chart on the US Billboard Hot 100 — the smooth, sun-soaked sound that defined Afrobeats' global breakthrough summer.

songs.title: Dancing Queen
songs.why: The crown jewel of Sweden's greatest export — ABBA turned Stockholm into a pop songwriting capital, a legacy that runs through Max Martin to today's charts.

songs.title: Wake Me Up
songs.why: Stockholm DJ Tim Bergling fused folk and EDM into one of the most-streamed songs ever — the sound of Sweden's world-conquering dance music scene.

songs.title: Three Little Birds
songs.why: Reggae's gospel of reassurance — 'every little thing gonna be all right' made a Kingston sound the island's gift to the world.

songs.title: One Love / People Get Ready
songs.why: Jamaica's unofficial anthem of unity, so central to national identity that the country's tourism board adopted it — reggae as a message of peace.

songs.title: De Música Ligera
songs.why: The most iconic song of rock en español, from Buenos Aires' legendary Soda Stereo — every Argentine crowd still sings its outro in one voice.

songs.title: Shakira: Bzrp Music Sessions, Vol. 53
songs.why: Bizarrap, a producer from Buenos Aires, turned bedroom sessions into the biggest franchise in Latin music — this one broke YouTube records within hours.

songs.title: Down Under
songs.why: A cheeky tour of Australian identity — Vegemite sandwiches and all — that became the country's unofficial anthem and a #1 hit worldwide.

songs.title: Thunderstruck
songs.why: Sydney's AC/DC are among the best-selling bands in history — stadium hard rock that made Australia a permanent fixture of the rock canon.

songs.title: Dance Monkey
songs.why: From busking on Byron Bay streets to the most-Shazamed song ever — a modern reminder of Australia's knack for left-field global hits.
